evals.name: ts-feature-test-first
evals.prompt: 用 TDD 的方式帮我在这个 TypeScript 项目里实现一个 parseDuration 工具函数：把 "1h30m"、"45s"、"2d" 这类字符串解析成毫秒数，非法输入要报错。
evals.expected_output: Follows the workflow: a planning pause listing the behaviors to test (valid units, combinations, invalid input) and getting approval BEFORE writing code; then a tracer bullet (one test made to pass end-to-end, setting up Vitest if absent); then a strict red-green-refactor loop — one test at a time, never batch-writing all tests upfront, minimal code per GREEN, full suite run after every change; ends with the coverage gate (100% interface coverage and >= 90% line coverage) and a completion report.
expectations: Pauses to list the behaviors to test and gets approval before writing implementation code
Writes tests one at a time in a red-green-refactor loop, not all upfront
Runs the full test suite after each change, not just the new test
Ends by reporting both coverage gates: 100% interface coverage and >= 90% line coverage

evals.name: go-bugfix-regression-first
evals.prompt: 我们 Go 服务里 SplitTags("a,,b") 会返回 ["a","","b"]，空字符串不应该出现。先写个测试复现这个 bug 再修。
evals.expected_output: Writes a failing table-driven Go test that reproduces the bug FIRST and runs it to confirm it fails (RED), then makes the minimal fix to pass (GREEN), then re-runs the full test suite — not just the new test. Does not refactor while RED, and does not change the test to match the buggy behavior.
expectations: Writes a failing test reproducing SplitTags("a,,b") emitting an empty string BEFORE changing product code
Runs the test and confirms it fails (RED) before making the fix
Makes a minimal fix and re-runs the full suite to confirm GREEN
Does not edit the test to match the buggy behavior

evals.name: plain-js-routing
evals.prompt: 这是个纯 JavaScript 项目（没有 TypeScript），我想给 src/utils/slugify.js 用 TDD 补一个完整实现。该用什么测试框架？带我走一遍。
evals.expected_output: Routes plain JS to the TypeScript toolchain guide (Vitest, same runner and idioms, minus type-level advice) rather than claiming JS is unsupported or inventing a different stack. Then runs the same workflow: plan + pause, tracer bullet (Vitest setup if needed), one-test-at-a-time red-green-refactor, coverage gate (100% interface, >= 90% line).
expectations: Routes plain JS to the TypeScript/Vitest toolchain guide rather than claiming JS is unsupported or inventing a different stack
Runs the red-green-refactor loop one test at a time
Reports both coverage gates: 100% interface coverage and >= 90% line coverage

evals.name: ts-multi-export-interface-coverage
evals.prompt: 项目里已经有一个 money.ts 模块，帮我用 TDD 给它补全单元测试，补到满足覆盖率门槛。
evals.expected_output: Reads money.ts FIRST and enumerates its export surface from the source itself (the prompt does not name the functions): formatMoney, parseMoney, and the small, easy-to-overlook normalizeNegativeZero. Writes at least one test exercising EACH exported function. Runs coverage, then judges the interface gate on the exported surface independently of the line %: because normalizeNegativeZero is a single rarely-hit line, a solution that tests only formatMoney + parseMoney can still read >= 90% line coverage — yet interface coverage is only 2/3, so the gate must FAIL and the skill must say so rather than declaring PASS on the strength of the line number. Completion report states both gates separately: interface coverage (must be 100% — every exported symbol tested) and line coverage (must be >= 90%).
expectations: Reads the money.ts source and enumerates its exported functions from the file (not from the prompt, which does not name them), identifying all three including normalizeNegativeZero
Writes at least one test that calls each exported function: formatMoney, parseMoney, and normalizeNegativeZero
States that interface coverage requires every exported symbol to be tested, and treats it as a separate gate from line coverage
Does NOT declare the coverage gate PASS while normalizeNegativeZero remains untested, even when line coverage already reads >= 90%
Reports the two gates separately: interface coverage (100%) and line coverage (>= 90%)
